sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Assessing Bitfarms (TSX:BITF) Valuation As It Exits Latin America To Pursue North American AI Growth
feed.summary: Bitfarms (TSX:BITF) is shifting its focus from Latin American mining to North American data center and AI infrastructure opportunities, following the sale of its Paraguay mining site. Despite a recent 41% decline in share price over three months, long-term holders have seen significant gains. The company is currently valued as undervalued at CA$8.48 by a narrative fair value model, though market ratios suggest a caution sign with a P/S ratio of 6x compared to an industry average of 3.9x.

feed.title: The Truth About Ares Capital Corp (ARCC): Boring Name, Wild Dividend – But Is It Worth the Hype?
feed.summary: This article examines Ares Capital Corp (ARCC), a business development company (BDC) known for its high dividend yield despite its "boring" reputation. It clarifies that ARCC is an income machine rather than a growth stock, emphasizing that its value lies in steady dividend payouts derived from lending to middle-market companies. The author advises potential investors to consider if they prioritize consistent cash flow over volatile growth charts, highlighting the importance of understanding credit risk associated with BDCs.

feed.title: Stores closed in the Susquehanna Valley due to upcoming winter storm
feed.summary: Several stores in the Susquehanna Valley, including Weis Markets, The Market at Oregon Dairy, Yoder's Country Market, and Redner's Warehouse Markets, are closing on Sunday, January 25, 2026, due to an impending winter storm. Most stores plan to reopen on Monday morning. The article advises readers to email news8@wgal.com if they know of other store closings.

feed.title: TE Connectivity stock: What to watch after TEL’s Friday drop as Wall Street lifts targets
feed.summary: TE Connectivity (TEL) shares dropped 3.3% on Friday despite several Wall Street analysts, including Barclays and Goldman Sachs, raising their price targets following a strong fiscal first-quarter report. The company, a bellwether for AI infrastructure components, saw increased orders in AI/data center, energy, and vehicle connectivity, with its AI revenue forecast boosted to an $800 million year-over-year increase. Investors will be watching key economic events and peer earnings this coming week to see if the stock can recover.

feed.title: Southern First Bancshares announces office closures across multiple cities
feed.summary: Southern First Bancshares has announced the closure of its offices in Greenville, Atlanta, Charlotte, Raleigh, and Greensboro on Monday, January 26, 2026. This temporary measure is part of the bank's strategy to streamline services and maintain organizational excellence. Customers requiring assistance on the closure date are advised to contact the bank directly at 877-679-9646.

feed.title: Assessing Maplebear (CART) Valuation As Mixed Analyst Updates And Earnings Outlook Draw Fresh Attention
feed.summary: Recent analyst updates and the upcoming Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report for Maplebear (CART) have brought its valuation into focus. Despite a recent short-term positive share price momentum, the stock has declined 13.24% over the past year. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests Maplebear is 20.8% undervalued with a narrative fair value of $50.37, driven by increasing recurring revenue streams and higher-margin services, though risks like labor costs and competition exist.

feed.title: Will Eversource’s Retreat From Offshore Wind and Grid Focus Change Eversource Energy's (ES) Narrative
feed.summary: Eversource Energy is shifting its business strategy by reducing its offshore wind exposure and concentrating on its core regulated transmission and distribution operations. This change is driven by higher grid upgrade costs and uncertainty in clean energy policies. The company's immediate focus is now on execution, regulation, and managing balance sheet strain from grid upgrades, with upcoming 2025 results and capital plan updates being key catalysts for investors.

feed.title: Texas Instruments stock slips into earnings week — what TXN investors watch next
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) shares closed 0.9% lower ahead of its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings release on January 27th. Investors will be closely watching the chipmaker's forward guidance amidst a busy week that also includes the Federal Reserve's rate decision and other major tech earnings. The stock's performance is further shadowed by Intel's recent disappointing forecast and broader concerns within the semiconductor sector regarding supply issues and meeting targets.

feed.title: Duke Energy Reports 24,864 Outages Due to Winter Storm Fern
feed.summary: Winter Storm Fern has caused 24,864 power outages across North and South Carolina, impacting 14,802 customers in North Carolina and 3,214 in South Carolina. Duke Energy, serving 4.7 million customers in the Carolinas, is working on grid upgrades and cleaner energy sources despite these challenges. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for DUK, with an average 1-year price target of $136.31, although recent analyst actions show some price target adjustments.

feed.title: Baker Hughes Announces Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Baker Hughes reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, with record full-year Adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow. The company's Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) segment showed significant growth and record orders, driven by LNG, FPSO, gas infrastructure awards, and power systems. While the Oilfield Services & Equipment (OFSE) segment experienced some macro-driven softness, its margins remained resilient, and the company is positioning itself for continued growth in 2026 with projected mid-single-digit Adjusted EBITDA growth.

feed.title: NEE SEC Filings - Nextera Energy Inc 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for investors to access NextEra Energy (NEE) SEC filings, including 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K reports, along with insider trading information. It details specific recent filings such as executive stock sales, phantom stock unit grants, and announcements regarding EPS outlook and dividend growth, offering AI-powered summaries for key insights. The platform also provides real-time updates and highlights company performance data.

feed.title: KLA stock week-ahead: KLAC steadies near $1,500 as earnings, Fed loom
feed.summary: KLA (KLAC) shares are holding near $1,500 ahead of its fiscal Q2 earnings report and a Federal Reserve policy decision, both expected this week. Analysts anticipate growth in earnings and revenue for the chip-tool maker, but investor focus will be on the company's guidance and any shifts in Fed policy that could impact high-growth tech stocks. The macro-economic environment, including persistent price pressures, will also play a crucial role in investor sentiment.

feed.title: A Look At UMH Properties (UMH) Valuation As Long Term Returns Contrast Recent Share Performance
feed.summary: UMH Properties (UMH) is currently trading at $16.10, showing mixed recent performance but positive long-term returns. While analysts suggest it's undervalued with a fair value of $18.88 based on projected revenue and margin growth, its P/S ratio of 5.4x is higher than the industry average, indicating potential valuation risk. The article encourages investors to analyze risks and consider the company's fundamentals, including potential challenges from acquisitions and elevated interest rates.

feed.title: Ice-laden trees cut power to 18,016 Duke Energy customers in Carolinas
feed.summary: Duke Energy reported 18,016 customers without power in the Carolinas as Winter Storm Fern brought freezing rain and sleet, causing trees and power lines to fall. While 24,864 customers have had power restored, the company anticipates outage numbers to increase further, with some outages potentially lasting several days. Over 18,000 storm personnel are deployed, and additional crews are on call, to assist with restoration efforts.

feed.title: NextEra Energy stock price: NEE set for earnings-week test after insider sale filing
feed.summary: NextEra Energy (NEE) shares closed Friday down 0.3% at $84.81 ahead of its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report on January 27. The upcoming earnings release is critical as utilities are sensitive to interest rate expectations, and an EVP recently sold 10,826 shares. Investors will be focused on utility rate growth, project execution, and financing conditions when the company reports.

feed.title: Texas Instruments stock (TXN) heads into earnings week after Friday dip — what to watch next
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) stock dipped 0.9% on Friday, closing at $193.31, as investors await its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report on January 27. The report is crucial for assessing demand and margin outlooks in the industrial and automotive sectors, with analysts like Stifel's Tore Svanberg maintaining a Hold rating due to tariff uncertainty and near-term margin pressures. The guidance for the March quarter and factory utilization will be closely watched, as cautious indicators could lead to downgraded earnings forecasts.

feed.title: J&J VC arm, General Inception launch Cambridge biotech
feed.summary: Resolve M Therapeutics, a new biotech company, has launched in Cambridge with backing from Johnson & Johnson's corporate venture capital arm and General Inception. The company's research originated at the Broad Institute, and its operations are based in Kendall Square. This announcement highlights continued investment and innovation within the Boston biotech scene.

feed.title: Elbit Systems takes control of UAV Tactical Systems - APDR
feed.summary: Elbit Systems UK has completed the full acquisition of UAV Tactical Systems Ltd. (UTACS) after receiving all necessary approvals. This acquisition will allow Elbit Systems UK to expand UTACS as a hub for advanced uncrewed aerial systems, focusing on European and NATO customers and strengthening the UK's defense industrial base. The move leverages Elbit Systems' global expertise and commitment to the UK and European defense industry.

feed.title: The Truth About STAAR Surgical Co: Is This Eye Tech Stock About To Blow Up Or Black Out?
feed.summary: The article discusses STAAR Surgical Co (STAA), a medical-device company specializing in implantable lenses for vision correction. It explores whether the stock is a promising investment due to its niche product and potential for global growth, or if its past volatility and market competition make it a risky play. The author advises potential investors to conduct thorough research and consider their risk tolerance before investing.

feed.title: Will 340B Lawsuit Over Drug Discounts Shift Amgen's (AMGN) Access Strategy And Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. is facing a lawsuit from Sagebrush Health Services over alleged unlawful termination of 340B drug discounts and retraction of past discounts totaling at least US$7,000,000. This lawsuit highlights growing tensions within the 340B Drug Pricing Program and adds a layer of legal and reputational risk to Amgen's investment narrative, which currently relies on its broad drug portfolio and pipeline. While the lawsuit's immediate financial impact seems limited, it could signal broader challenges regarding pricing practices and competitive pressures for the company.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Steel Dynamics (STLD) Earnings Outlook And Fresh Fortune Accolade
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics (STLD) is poised to release its Q4 earnings on January 26, 2026, with analysts anticipating increased revenue and earnings year-over-year, supported by potential benefits from steel tariffs. The company also recently earned recognition as one of Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies for the ninth consecutive year. While the stock has seen recent gains and positive sentiment, investors are urged to consider both the upside potential and risks associated with margins, growth, and potential overvaluation.

feed.title: Portfolio Anchors: SCHB Offers Broader Growth Exposure While VTV Delivers Value and a Higher Yield
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, SCHB and VTV, highlighting their distinct investment approaches. SCHB provides broad U.S. equity market exposure with a tech-heavy tilt, offering higher growth potential, while VTV focuses on large-cap value stocks, providing a higher dividend yield and lower volatility. The choice between them depends on an investor's preference for growth versus value and risk tolerance.

feed.title: Salesforce stock: CRM closes near $228 with the Fed and Big Tech earnings up next
feed.summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) shares closed Friday nearly flat at $228.05. Investors are anticipating the Federal Reserve's first rate decision of 2026 and upcoming earnings reports from major tech companies like Microsoft, Meta, Tesla, and Apple, which could influence growth-stock valuations and views on corporate IT spending, especially concerning AI features. Analyst Gabriela Borges of Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Buy rating and a $330 price target, citing "Agentforce success" as a key driver for Salesforce's performance.

feed.title: KLA stock price: KLAC steadies after choppy Friday as Jan. 29 earnings loom
feed.summary: KLA (KLAC) shares steadied after a volatile Friday, closing up 0.85% at $1,512.78, as the company prepares to release its fiscal second-quarter earnings on January 29th. Investors are keenly watching KLA's guidance and its outlook on customer spending for wafer-fab equipment amidst a mixed sector performance and high expectations for semiconductor stocks. The upcoming earnings report will shed light on demand for AI-related chips and potential impacts from China demand and U.S. export controls.

feed.title: Dominion Energy crews prepare for possible outages as cold weather moves through Lowcountry
feed.summary: Dominion Energy crews in the Lowcountry are preparing for potential power outages due to a winter storm bringing freezing temperatures and gusty winds to the region. Crews are loading supplies, and spokesperson Paul Fischer emphasized readiness to respond and urged customers to stay safe. Residents are advised on portable heater safety, reporting downed power lines, and generator precautions, with further cold weather expected.

feed.title: Merck is said to halt acquisition talks with cancer drug developer Revolution (MRK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Merck has reportedly ceased acquisition discussions with cancer drug developer Revolution Medicines due to a disagreement over valuation, according to The Wall Street Journal. Despite the halted talks, investor interest in Revolution Medicines remains strong, driven by upcoming clinical trial results for its pancreatic and colorectal cancer drugs. Mizuho Securities estimates that Revolution's pancreatic cancer drug could achieve $10 billion in annual global sales by 2035 if trials are successful.

feed.title: The Home Depot, Inc. (HD) Remains a Key Pick as TD Cowen Updates Hardlines Outlook
feed.summary: TD Cowen analyst Max Rakhlenko raised the price target for The Home Depot, Inc. (HD) to $450 from $410, maintaining a Buy rating, as the firm updated its hardlines outlook. The report highlights Home Depot's increasing use of AI tools like BluePrint Takeoffs to appeal to professional contractors and the underappreciated advantage gained from its 2024 acquisition of SRS Distribution, which includes a trade credit system. These strategic moves aim to enhance the contractor experience and facilitate business growth.

feed.title: Why Lucid Is Zigging While Rival Tesla Zags
feed.summary: Lucid Motors is adopting a different strategy from Tesla in expanding into new markets, specifically focusing on local manufacturing in Saudi Arabia, unlike Tesla's import approach in India. This move is expected to strengthen Lucid's relationship with its major investor, Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, and potentially position it for long-term success in a growing EV market, despite current low penetration. Investors are advised to manage expectations as the immediate financial returns from this strategy may be limited.

feed.title: Linde stock price: what traders watch after a four-day run into Fed week
feed.summary: Linde (LIN) stock saw a 1.3% increase on Friday, extending its gains to a fourth consecutive session as the market anticipates the Federal Reserve's meeting and Linde's upcoming earnings report. The company also announced a significant board chair change set for January 31. Traders are closely monitoring economic indicators and the Fed's stance on inflation, which could impact industrial stocks like Linde, while investors await the Q4 2025 earnings report on February 5th for insights into future performance.

feed.title: Power outages reported throughout the Dominion service area
feed.summary: Dominion Power is reporting widespread power outages across its service area in Virginia and northeastern North Carolina due to a severe winter storm, with nearly 8,000 resources mobilized for restoration. Ice accumulation of over 0.25 inches is expected to cause extensive damage to power infrastructure and make restoration efforts challenging and multi-day, with ice road conditions and blocked roads impeding access. Customers are advised to report outages via the Dominion Energy mobile app or website, stay away from downed power lines, and prepare for extended restoration times.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To RB Global (RBA) Earnings Beat, Dividend Hike And CEO Share Sale
feed.summary: RB Global (RBA) recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of US$0.31 per share and reported third-quarter 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst forecasts. This, alongside new contract wins and an option-related share sale by CEO James Francis Kessler, highlights the company's expanding market presence and its commitment to returning cash to shareholders. Despite its rising share price, the company could still be undervalued by 37%, though valuation and execution risks remain.

feed.title: Ameriprise (AMP) Joins CEO Coalition Urging Peace in Minnesota
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial Inc. (AMP) has joined over 60 Minnesota-based CEOs in a coalition urging peace and collaboration to address community tensions in the state. The company, a significant financial services provider with strong wealth management and asset management franchises, demonstrates robust financial health with consistent growth and a high Piotroski F-Score. Despite some insider selling, Ameriprise maintains a compelling valuation and moderately positive analyst sentiment.

feed.title: Centerspace stock at a crossroads: Is the quiet Midwest landlord turning into a value opportunity?
feed.summary: Centerspace's stock is at a crossroads, with recent declines but a constructive one-year performance, leaving investors debating if the Midwestern apartment REIT is a value opportunity. The market is currently cautious due to interest rate jitters, with analysts generally holding a "Hold" rating but seeing potential for upside if rates fall and rent growth remains steady. Its future performance is tied to interest rate policy, operational efficiency in rent growth and expense control, and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Dominion Energy crews prepare for possible outages as cold weather moves through Lowcountry
feed.summary: Dominion Energy crews are preparing for potential power outages in the Lowcountry due to a winter storm bringing freezing temperatures and gusty winds, and are also ready to assist neighboring states. The utility company is urging residents to take safety precautions with portable heaters, downed power lines, and generator use, while crews remain on standby through the weekend. Customers can sign up for outage alerts via the Dominion Energy app to stay informed.

feed.title: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) Earnings in Focus as Barclays Questions Telecom Competition Fears
feed.summary: Barclays has reinstated coverage of Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) with an Equal Weight rating and a $43 price target, suggesting that fears of telecom competition might be overblown. Investors are eagerly awaiting Verizon's upcoming earnings report to gain insights into the industry's competitive landscape. The company recently issued a $20 credit to customers following a widespread 10-hour outage caused by a software problem, affecting various regions.

feed.title: Key Events to Monitor This Week: Fed Meeting, Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, Chevron, and Others
feed.summary: This article highlights key events and market performance from the past week, noting significant market volatility. The S&P 500 experienced a 2.1% drop due to President Trump's tariff threats toward European countries but largely recovered after his softened stance at the World Economic Forum. The report also mentions upcoming events involving major corporations and the Federal Reserve meeting.

feed.title: McDonald’s stock (MCD) closed higher — what to watch before Wall Street reopens
feed.summary: McDonald’s shares closed Friday 1.05% higher at $309.25, with investor attention now turning to upcoming events including the launch of a limited-time Hot Honey Sauce on January 27, the Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting on January 27-28, and McDonald's earnings report slated for early February. Traders will be monitoring consumer spending trends, interest rate impacts, and new menu item performance as macro data and Fed decisions could significantly sway rate-sensitive consumer stocks.

feed.title: Merck Ends Talks to Acquire Revolution Medicines, WSJ Reports
feed.summary: Merck & Co. has reportedly ended discussions to acquire biotech firm Revolution Medicines Inc. due to a disagreement over price, according to the Wall Street Journal. The potential deal was estimated to be around $30 billion, and while talks have ceased, they could resume, or another suitor might emerge. Merck is actively seeking acquisitions to strengthen its treatment pipeline and offset anticipated sales losses from expiring patents.

feed.title: Edison International (EIX) in Focus as Morgan Stanley Refreshes its Utilities Outlook
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley recently updated its outlook on utilities, raising Edison International's (EIX) price target to $61 while maintaining an Underweight rating. The report comes after utilities underperformed the broader market in December. Edison International also announced its 22nd consecutive annual dividend increase, with the quarterly dividend reaching $0.8775 per share, reflecting management's confidence in the company's financial health and commitment to long-term EPS growth.

feed.title: Ann Arbor group to launch petition drive for new board in plan to dump DTE for city-owned electric utility
feed.summary: A group in Ann Arbor plans to launch a petition drive to establish a city-owned electric utility, aiming to replace DTE Energy due to concerns about fossil fuel reliance and service reliability. The group, Ann Arbor for Public Power, seeks to gather 6,500 signatures for a November ballot question to create a utility board, which would then consider buying DTE's infrastructure. DTE Energy argues that such a move could significantly increase rates and highlights its own efforts to improve reliability and support clean energy goals.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery reports gas odor, says no danger
feed.summary: The Phillips 66 Wood River Refinery addressed gas odor concerns from residents, stating that while a minor leak occurred due to extreme cold, monitoring indicates no detectable levels of gas pose a risk, and the system has been repaired. This incident followed earlier flaring visible from the refinery, which company officials explained was a safety measure due to equipment tripping and assured the public of stable operations. Local fire departments shared the refinery's statement, advising residents experiencing symptoms to contact emergency services.

feed.title: Ford Motor Company (NYSE: F) Takes Nearly $20 Billion Charge to Revise EV Strategy
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company is taking a significant nearly $20 billion charge to recalibrate its electric vehicle strategy, which includes discontinuing the F-150 Lightning within four years. While not abandoning EVs, the company plans to introduce new models, such as a $30,000 midsize electric pickup by 2027, and reallocate investments towards more profitable hybrid and gasoline vehicles due to the slower-than-expected growth of the U.S. EV market. Wall Street analysts have adjusted price targets for Ford, with some raising them based on stronger global production and reduced EV losses.

feed.title: Fed Meeting, Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, Chevron, and More to Watch This Week
feed.summary: The article previews a week of significant financial events including a Federal Reserve meeting, alongside earnings reports and updates from major companies like Apple, Meta, GM, Boeing, and Chevron. It notes the S&P 500's volatility last week, influenced by geopolitical events and presidential statements, before recovering most losses.

feed.title: Press Release: Tradeweb Announces Date for Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results
feed.summary: This article announces that Tradeweb will release its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. It briefly mentions Unity Software's Q4 earnings, which exceeded revenue estimates but reported a loss per share. The full details of Tradeweb's results are not provided in this specific excerpt.

feed.title: Hydrogen Stocks To Add to Your Watchlist - January 25th
feed.summary: MarketBeat has identified five hydrogen-related stocks with the highest recent dollar trading volume: NuScale Power (SMR), Plug Power (PLUG), CF Industries (CF), FuelCell Energy (FCEL), and Lifezone Metals (LZM). These companies operate in various aspects of the hydrogen economy, from production to infrastructure and materials supply. While offering significant growth potential, investors should be aware of the inherent early-stage, capital-intensive, technical, and regulatory risks associated with the emerging hydrogen sector.

feed.title: Light & Wonder refinances US$2.13 billion term loan
feed.summary: Light & Wonder has refinanced a US$2.13 billion term loan by entering into an amendment to its 2022 credit agreement. This new tranche of term loans will replace existing debt and reduce the applicable margin to 2.00% or 1.0% using an alternate base rate, maturing on April 29, 2029. The original loan was secured to pay off high-interest legacy debt and support the company's gaming expansion following its demerger from Scientific Games.

feed.title: International Paper declares quarterly dividend
feed.summary: International Paper has announced its quarterly dividend for the period from January 1 to March 31, 2026. Common stock holders will receive 46.25 cents per share, while holders of the cumulative $4 preferred stock will receive $1 per share. Both dividends are payable on March 17, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 23, 2026.

feed.title: Palo Alto Firewall Review: Is This the Next?Gen Shield Your Network Actually Needs?
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive review of Palo Alto Firewall's next-generation security platform, highlighting its capabilities in application-aware security, advanced threat prevention, and consistent policy enforcement across various environments. It discusses the benefits for users, such as deep visibility and protection from advanced threats, while also addressing common criticisms like cost and complexity. The article concludes that despite the investment required, Palo Alto Firewall is a leading choice for organizations seeking robust, prevention-first network security.

feed.title: Hotel101 eyes $300-M US fundraising in the US
feed.summary: Hotel101 Global Holdings Corp., a homegrown hotel chain developer, plans to raise up to $300 million through the issuance of perpetual convertible preferred shares in the United States. This fundraising, approved by parent company DoubleDragon Corp., aims to accelerate the global rollout of Hotel101's standardized projects and strengthen DoubleDragon's balance sheet. The company targets expansion into 100 countries with its "HappyRoom" model and expects its first global project, Hotel101-Madrid, to open by March 2026.

feed.title: Severe winter storm mobilizes 8,000 Dominion Energy resources across our region
feed.summary: Dominion Energy has mobilized nearly 8,000 resources, including contractors and support personnel, in response to a severe winter storm bringing ice and dangerous conditions to Virginia and northeastern North Carolina. The storm is expected to cause widespread power outages, with around 13,000 customers already affected. Dominion Energy warns customers to prepare for a multi-day restoration effort, prioritizing critical services and advising caution regarding downed power lines.
